reasoning: Both models correctly identified the PKGBUILD as safe: it downloads from official CDN with checksum verification, uses standard packaging patterns, and contains no dangerous operations during build/install. The skipped AppImage isn't executed during makepkg.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped review of Lunar%20Client-3.7.7-ow.AppImage binary
No review of potential .install hooks (though none exist in this package)
false_negatives: AppImage binary skipped entirely - while not executed during makepkg, it could theoretically contain malicious payloads that activate at runtime (mitigated by checksum verification)
learnings: Improve handling of binaries: Flag if binaries are executed during build (this AppImage is only extracted)
Prioritize reviewing any .install files before binaries
Enhance prompts to recognize safe AppImage extraction patterns
Implement checksum validation awareness in model reasoning
